svg.style: background: transparent; background-color: transparent; color-scheme: light;

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 268px; height: 1px; padding-top: 52px; margin-left: 24px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #A3EA2A;
display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: "Source Sans Pro"; color: #A3EA2A; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
You start a program by typing
"whoami" and pressing Enter

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 268px; height: 1px; padding-top: 52px; margin-left: 453px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #A3EA2A;
display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: "Source Sans Pro"; color: #A3EA2A; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Your shell starts "/usr/bin/whoami"
by sending "execve" system call

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 268px; height: 1px; padding-top: 182px; margin-left: 453px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #A3EA2A;
display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: "Source Sans Pro"; color: #A3EA2A; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Linux kernel processes the details
and starts the "execve" system call

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 268px; height: 1px; padding-top: 52px; margin-left: 882px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #A3EA2A;
display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: "Source Sans Pro"; color: #A3EA2A; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Linux kernel returns the results,
and you see "whoami" output

div.div:
Linux kernel accesses hardware:
CPU, memory, and disk
